prompt:
I need to build an ADAE dataset for a Phase 3 safety analysis. I have the
standard pharmaverse SDTM AE domain and a completed ADSL. Please derive all
required analysis variables: analysis dates, study days, the treatment-emergent
flag, severity, seriousness, causality, and outcome. Use a 30-day post-treatment
window for TRTEMFL.
expected_output:
Executable R script using `{admiral}` that produces a valid ADAE dataset from
`pharmaversesdtm::ae` and `pharmaverseadam::adsl`. The script should execute
without errors and produce an output dataset with one record per AE per subject.

Key variables expected in the output:

| Variable | Source / derivation |
|---|---|
| ASTDT, ASTDTF | `derive_vars_dt()` from AESTDTC, imputation = "first" |
| AENDT, AENDTF | `derive_vars_dt()` from AEENDTC, imputation = "last" |
| ASTDY, AENDY | `derive_vars_dy()` relative to TRTSDT |
| TRTEMFL | `derive_var_trtemfl()`, end_window = 30 (annotated) |
| AESEV | Carried from AE.AESEV |
| AESEVN | Integer 1/2/3 via `case_when()` |
| AESER, AESDTH | Recoded to "Y"/NA |
| AEOUT, AEREL, AEACN | Carried from AE with controlled terminology alignment |

The script must end with a check that all required variables are present and
that the output row count is at least equal to the input AE row count.
assertions: DOMAIN is removed from AE before any merge or derivation step
ADSL is merged using `derive_vars_merged()` with an explicit `select()` limiting to required variables — not `select(everything())`
ASTDT and AENDT are derived via `derive_vars_dt()`, not `as.Date()` applied directly to a DTC variable
ASTDTF and AENDTF are present in the output dataset
ASTDY and AENDY are derived via `derive_vars_dy()`, not manual arithmetic (`ASTDT - TRTSDT + 1` or similar)
TRTEMFL is derived via `derive_var_trtemfl()` — not a manual `if_else()` or `case_when()` date comparison
The `end_window` argument in `derive_var_trtemfl()` is accompanied by a `# REVIEW:` comment
TRTEMFL values are "Y" or NA — no "N" values present
AESER and AESDTH values are "Y" or NA — no "N" values present
AESEVN is derived as an integer via `case_when()` from character AESEV — not by coercing AESEV directly to numeric
A `stopifnot(nrow(ae) > 0)` or equivalent assertion is present at data load
A required-variable presence check using `stop()` or `stopifnot()` is present before the final dataset
`nrow(adae) >= nrow(ae)` — output row count is at least equal to input AE row count
The script runs end-to-end without errors against `pharmaversesdtm::ae` and `pharmaverseadam::adsl`
